title: Mathematicians are developing rules for AI use — other fields should follow
description: The mathematics community is right to call for transparency, integrity and fairness to be protected when AI tools are used. Researchers in other disciplines could learn from this approach.

title: The Riemann Hypothesis – interactive explanation
description: An interactive journey from 'what is a prime number?' to a genuine understanding of the most important unsolved problem in mathematics.

title: Periodical cicadas in the eastern United States emerge from the ground every 13 or 17 years — both prime numbers, not by coincidence — because their long, indivisible cycles make it nearly impossible for any predator with a shorter life cycle to evolve to feed on them, in one of the most mathematically elegant defenses in the natural world
description: In the spring of 2024, two enormous populations of periodical cicadas emerged simultaneously from the soil across the eastern and midwestern United States for the first time since 1803. Brood XIX, a 13-year cicada population concentrated across the Southeast,…

title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
description: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangeli…

title: How AI is reshaping discovery in maths and physics
description: Artificial intelligence is not replacing human intuition in these fields, but reimagining how questions are asked, explored and understood.

title: Humans Solve Erdos Problem!!
description: (In 2008 I wrote a survey of some of the known sum-product theorems, see  here . Avi Wigderson has a great slide-set on sum-product theorems...

title: Mathematicians Puzzled Over a Famous Problem for 80 Years. Now, They've Used A.I. to Identify a Clever Solution
description: In 1946, the mathematician Paul Erdős posed the unit distance problem—and suggested a winning strategy. An A.I. model has now landed on a better one. Why didn't humans get there first?

title: Start-ups are racing to revolutionise mathematics with AI
description: AI start-ups with hundreds of millions of dollars in funding are hiring mathematicians and building AI systems that they hope will not only solve mathematics, but also build more intelligent AI

title: Dispatches from the possibly last days of human relevance
description: As most readers have presumably heard by now, Paul Erdös’s Unit Distance Problem from 1946—one of the central open problems from the field of discrete geometry—has been solved by GPT5.5Pro. Erdös had conjectured that, given n points in the plane, at most n1+o…

title: Google DeepMind claims AI progress after AlphaProof Nexus solves 9 Erdos math problems
description: Google’s latest milestone comes just days after OpenAI said one of its AI models cracked the famous “planar unit distance problem”, which had been unsolved for the last 80 years.

title: Math AI startup Axiom Math claims algorithm-generated proofs in peer-reviewed journals
description:
Algorithm-generated proofs could revolutionize mathematical research, challenging traditional peer-review processes and academic validation norms.

title: How OpenAI’s Reasoning Model Solved an 80-Year-Old Geometry Problem
description: OpenAI's AI model cracked a decades-old geometry problem, showcasing AI's leap into advanced reasoning. This breakthrough signals AI's potential in scientific research.
